free_text: 200 and 300 MeV/nucleon nuclear reactions responsible
for single-event effects in microelectronics.
H.Niewodniczanski Institute of Nuclear
Physics, Cracow, Poland
T.J.Watson Research Center,NY, USA
The hydrogen jet target was approx. 5 x 10**27/cm2/s.
The experiment was carried out at the
CELSIUS storage ring of the Svedberg Laboratory,
Uppsala, Sweden.
Secondary particles were registered by four
detector systems-
The small angle detector(SAD) detects fragments from
the Si-28 beam and consists of two quadrants with 16
circular and 16 radial 300-mum Si strip detectors(SISD)
in front of an 8-mm plastic scintillator.
The forward wall detector(FWD) consists of 24 750-mum
Si detectors followed by 1-mm fast plastic scintillator
glued on top of 80-mm-long CsI crystals(CSICR).
The zero angle detector(ZAD) is a telescope comprising
two SISD and a plastic scintillator.
The spectator tagging detector(STD).
Normalized to He fragment production cross sections
predicted by Dubna Cascade Model at 4.9 deg
.The Dubna cascade model and JAERI version of the
quantum molecular dynamics model.
The letter was sent to authors
(henrik.jaderstrom@tsl.uu.se) 16.10.2008. No reply.
SB

REACTION code (in coincidence  with He detection)
Instead of COMMENT information added to MONITOR with
corrections. ANG-NRM, ELEM-NRM added in COMMON.
Subents 002,003 deleted (data are not experimental)
REACTION code in Subents 014,015 corrected.
SD: Phrase in MONITOR was changed.
